在匹诺康尼，不管要去哪里，都可以手捧一桶爆米花以作消遣。
No matter where you go in Penacony, you can hold a bucket of popcorn as a pastime.
门口贴着这样的标语：「再强调一次，这里不提供打手服务！！」
A sign on the door reads: "To emphasize, we do not provide thug-for-hire services!!"
匹诺康尼街边的自助餐车。不知为何总有人试图闯进车厢，看看里面究竟有什么。
A roadside self-serve food truck in Penacony. For some reason, people keep trying to barge into the car to see what is inside.
钟表小子专属周边贩售店，据说某些纪念品的材料直接取自钟表小子雕像。
Exclusive Clockie merchandise shop. Rumor has it that certain merch are made from materials directly harvested from the Clockie Statue.
匹诺康尼街边的自助商车。不知为何总有人试图闯进车厢，看看里面究竟有什么。
A roadside self-serve shopping truck in Penacony. For some reason, people keep trying to barge into the car to see what is inside.
各色商品在灯光下闪闪发亮，透过橱窗，你看见了精心包裹的欲望。
Various bits of merchandise glimmer under the light. Through the window, you see meticulously wrapped desire.
这家店铺卖的东西和对门完全相同，其中竞争想必非常激烈。
This store sells the same exact merchandise as the one opposite it. The competition must be fierce between the two.
这橱窗让你回忆起某个童话故事——你不禁开始想象，要是点燃一根火柴、或者捏碎一个梦泡，里面的东西会不会活过来。
This display sparks your memory of a fairy tale. You cannot help but imagine — what if you light a match, or crush a dream bubble? Will the things inside come to life?
音乐，欢笑，咖啡的清香，杯盏轻碰的脆响…哪怕不曾亲眼所见，你也能猜出门后的景象是多么有格调。
Music, laughter, the aroma of coffee, and the crisp clinking of cups... Even if not seen by your own eyes, it is not difficult to imagine the elegant scene behind the doors.
平平无奇的面包房，却是能给所有人带来幸福和满足的心灵港湾。
An ordinary bakery. Yet, it is a haven that brings happiness and warmth to everyone's souls.
即便隔着橱窗，你也能看到香甜、嗅到柔软、品尝到色泽金黄。
Even through the display windows, you can see the sweetness, smell the softness, and taste the golden hue.
面包，最适合果腹的食品之一。它在梦里远比现实中香甜许多。
Bread, one of the most suitable food to fill the stomach. It is even more delicious in the dream than in reality.
这家店铺贩卖的东西弥足珍贵，唯有数目庞大的金钱才能衡量。
This store sells items so precious that they can only be valued with a large sum of money.
奢侈品是种奇妙的东西：它们身价高昂，却从不拒绝被人拥有。
Luxury goods are truly wonderful. They carry a hefty price tag, but are still willing to be owned by people.
出奇豪华的餐厅。在梦中用餐不会有饱腹感，却能完整体验食物的美味——真是一举两得。
An astoundingly luxurious restaurant. You'll never feel full when you eat in the dream, yet you can fully experience the flavors of food — it kills two birds with one stone.
幽鲁士餐饮连锁的分店之一，家庭宴会和朋友小聚的不二选择。
One of Yuluxe's Culinary Chain's branches. It's the top choice for family and friend gatherings.
链接梦境与现实的银行。当口袋里的钞票见底时，你可以拜托他们帮你从现实取些过来……
A bank that is both connected to dream and reality. When your pockets run out of money, you can ask them to transfer money from reality...
不是所有来宾都能用到这项服务——因为大家在现实里也未必有钱。
Not all guests are able to use this service — as not everyone has money in reality.
报社，出产新闻，产出真相。
A newspaper publisher. They produce news and disseminate the truth.
每个梦境时刻都有专门负责传播好事的报社，此为其中之一。
Every Dreamscape Moment has a newspaper publisher dedicated to spreading good news — this is one of them.
挂在门口的标语：我们制造真理。
A slogan hangs on the door: We create the truth.
设立在桥下的店铺，远离大众视线，又不过分偏僻——是秘密集会的绝佳地点。
A store under the bridge, far from public eyes, but not too obscure. A perfect place for secret gatherings.
门后似乎是某个高档会所，玻璃的防窥探效果很好，在街上看不出太多端倪。
Behind the door seems to be some kind of upscale club. The glass is coated with a security layer that prevents prying eyes. You can't see much of it from the street.
「人靠衣装车靠胎。」只要走进这家店，丑小鸭也能变成黑天鹅。
"Clothes make the man and tires make the car." If you walk into this store, even an ugly duckling can also transform into a swan.
橱窗里琳琅满目的服饰预示了这家店铺不菲的消费。
The displays are full of dazzling clothes that imply the extravagant prices in the store.
鸢尾花家系旗下的娱乐工作室，无数稚嫩梦想的起点与终点。
The entertainment studio under the Iris Family — the beginning and end of many naive dreams.
知更鸟的故事鼓舞了许多人，让他们以为自己能像雏鸟一样飞翔——事实证明，雏鸟总是会跌落的。
Robin's story has inspired many, making them think that they could soar into the sky as a nestling... When in fact a baby bird will surely fall to the ground.
为普通人提供服务的苏乐达俱乐部。相比于饮料本身，「邂逅」才是这里更珍贵的商品。
A SoulGlad club that provides services for ordinary people. Even more than the beverage itself, "encounters" are a much more precious commodity here.
一家专供人类的苏乐达俱乐部。里面的顾客身高正常，没有尾巴或长耳，身体也不是铁皮构成的。
A SoulGlad club that primarily serves humans. Its customers are of usual height, have no tails nor long ears, and are not constructed from steel.
身高<unbreak>1.3</unbreak>米以上不得入内！
No entry for individuals over <unbreak>1.3</unbreak>m tall!
会所内免费供应苏乐达——再强调一遍，这里都是成年人！
SoulGlad is provided at no charge in the club — I repeat, only adults around here!
专供皮皮西的苏乐达俱乐部，不过它的门对皮皮西来说有些太高了。
A SoulGlad club that primarily serves Pepeshi, though the door is way too tall for them.
这是一家外貌调整店，可以让你像某些游戏的开头一样随心所欲地修改形象——当然，仅限梦中。
This is an appearance adjustment shop that allows you to tweak your looks any way you want, just like at the beginning of certain games... Only effective in the dream, of course.
「…为了让您回到现实后不会对自己的外观感到失望，我们贴心地关闭了『外貌调整』功能。」
...To ensure you do not feel disappointed in your looks when you return to reality, we have kindly turned off the Appearance Adjustment function.
这是一家「再次体验店」，可以让你重温梦境中最难忘的经历。
This is a Re-Experience shop, where you can relive your most memorable experiences in the Dreamscape.
梦中总有一些经历让你没齿难忘，这家店铺旨在帮助人们像复习书本一样重温那份心动。
Surely there are some episodes that you simply could not forget in your dream — this shop helps people re-live those tender emotions as if they were revising a book.
所有城市都需要一座酒店来见证历史，「黄金的时刻」也不例外。
All cities need a hotel to witness history, and the Golden Hour is no exception.
如果你想在梦中享受豪华的酒店套房，这会是你的不二之选。
If you wish to enjoy a luxurious hotel suite in your dream, there is no better choice.
不同于「白日梦」酒店，这家酒店的套房内既没有入梦池、也没有床。
Different from The Reverie, there are no Dreampool nor beds in this hotel's suite.
得体的客人们围坐在玻璃窗后的圆桌前。当需要谈论秘密时，他们便会拉起窗帘——看来今天的秘密有点多。
Respectable guests sit before the round table behind the glass. When they must discuss secrets, they will pull down the curtain. It appears there are many secrets today.
装修漂亮的店往往意味着里面的商品价格不菲，看来大部分人注定与这里无缘。
A shop with gorgeous decor usually suggests that its prices are quite dear — it looks like most people are not destined to enter this place.
厄皮洛斯服装秀场的分店，仅为VIP会员提供服务。店面不大，恰好能容下那些口袋阔绰的贵宾。
Branch outlet of the Epirus Apparel Fashion Show that only caters to its VIP members. The shop isn't spacious — just enough to host distinguished customers with deep pockets.
作为匹诺康尼的文化符号，苏乐达几乎可以出现在任何店铺的招牌上，而无需与商品关联——就比如这其实是一家潮玩店。
As a cultural symbol of Penacony, SoulGlad can show up on almost any store's sign without any association with its products. For example, this shop is actually a pop gadget store.
一家潮玩店，找不到标识，也没有任何宣传招牌。
A pop gadget where no logo can be found, and is devoid of any promotional signs.
这是一家典当铺，可用珍贵的物品或情绪作抵押。梦中总是有人需要这类服务。
This is a pawnshop where precious items or emotions can be used as collateral. There will always be people who need such services in the dream.
这家店铺不售卖商品——它只负责把珍贵之物换成钱财，好让那些不愿离开的穷光蛋在梦中多待些时日。
This shop does not sell items — it is only responsible for trading valuable items for money, allowing the poor who do not wish to leave to spend a few more days in the dream.
人类、皮皮西、智械…这座大型俱乐部欢迎每一位喜爱苏乐达的宾客。这就是匹诺康尼的精神——来者不拒。
Humans, Pepeshi, Intellitrons... This massive club welcomes every guest who loves SoulGlad. This is the spirit of Penacony — All are welcome.
格拉克斯大街上最具规模的苏乐达俱乐部，足以接待每一位奇形怪状的宾客。
The largest SoulGlad club along Glaux Avenue, enough to host every odd and eccentric customer that comes through its doors.
苏乐达俱乐部的后门，一些注重私密性的顾客会从这里悄悄进出。
The SoulGlad Club's back door. Customers who value their privacy will enter or slip out through this exit.
有时这扇门会迎接一些着装不算体面的顾客，有时它会用来销毁秘密。
This door is sometimes used to welcome more unsavory customers. Other times, it is used to destroy secrets.
当你靠近这扇门时，一段旋律突然从门后传来……
As you near this door, a melody can suddenly be heard from behind it...
你顿时感觉像被微风吹拂。空气中泛着咖啡的焦香，令你嘴里生涎。
You instantly feel like you're being blown at by a breeze. The air is tinged with a faint burnt scent of coffee, making your mouth salivate.
柜台上的表盘躁动起来，指针开始飞速旋转，仿佛要把你带到遥远过去的某个时刻……
The dial on the counter is stirred into life. Its needle starts to spin quickly as if it is about to take you to a distant moment in the past...
表盘
Dial
「为什么，为什么事情会变成这样……」
Why? Why has it come to this?
表盘
Dial
「冷静…现在只有我了…必须完成他留下的嘱托…我必须做到……」
"Calm down... It's just me left now... I have to complete the instructions he left behind... I have to do it..."
表盘
Dial
「可是…我该怎么做到…我怎么可能做到？」
"But... how do I do it... How can I possibly complete it?"
表盘
Dial
「唉…难道没人能拉我一把吗？」
"*sigh*... Is there no one who can help me?"
靠近这面书架时，你闻到了令人平静的木头气息——不是活的，而是原生的、经过碾压和漂白的木制纸张。
When you near this bookshelf, you catch a whiff of a wood scent that soothes the nerves — not alive, but all-natural, compressed, and bleached paper made from wood.
即便以梦境的尺度来看，这里的藏书数量也堪称夸张，仿佛过往历史的丰碑。
Even by Dreamscape measurements, the number of books stashed here border on hyperbole, as if monuments to history.
在万千书籍的注视下，你心底萌发出了一股愉悦的暖流，令你毛孔大开……
Under the gaze of these tens of thousands of books, a warm and joyful stream radiates from your inner self, making your pores expand...
随着你的靠近，积木们纷纷躁动起来。它们发出声音，如同一千张嘴同时开口……
As you get closer, the densely packed blocks start to stir. They make a sound akin to a thousand mouths opening at once...
积木
Building Blocks
「看啊，我们坚固如山。再激烈的玩闹也不足以让我们倒塌。」
"Look here, we are immovable like a mountain. Even the most intense shakedowns will not topple us."
积木
Building Blocks
「可是没了玩伴，这一切还有什么意义？」
"But what's the point of everything if we have no playmates?"
积木
Building Blocks
「不会回来了…他被遗弃了，所以我们也被抛下……」
"He won't return... He has been discarded, and so have we..."
隐约间，你似乎听到积木们在低声啜泣。但当你想要仔细去听时，一切声音都消失了。
Very faintly, you seem to hear the blocks whimpering softly. But when you want to listen more closely, all sound has vanished.
柔软的沙发。上面可能承载过某些柔软的记忆。
A very soft sofa. Many soft memories may have been borne on it.
表皮光滑而完整，可以肯定的是，房间的主人绝对没有在这养过宠物。
Its surface is glossy and flawless. It is most certain that the master of this room never kept pets.
舒适无比的沙发，一不留神就会让你浪费数个小时。
An extremely comfy sofa. You'll waste many hours lounging on it if you're not paying attention.
你随手抽出本书、翻开，里面的字符模糊成一片，即便是联觉信标也识别不出来。
You casually pull out a book and flip it open — the characters in it are so smudged that even a Synesthesia Beacon cannot comprehend them.
你试图拿起旁边的笔，在纸上留下几句记录，却怎么也写不出能辨认的文字。
You try to pick up a nearby pen to write a few notes on the paper but realize that you can't even write in any legible language.
你看完了书，却怎么也记不住它的内容——据说人从梦中醒来后也是如此。
You finish reading the book, but somehow can't remember its contents. It's said that this phenomenon is the same for someone who has just woken up from a dream.
两人份的下午茶——会是谁呢？现在他们又在哪里？
Afternoon tea for two — who could they have been? Where have they gone?
桌上的茶杯空空如也，里面的液体已经干涸。看来这里已经荒废了很长时间。
The teacups on the table are empty, the liquid inside long dried. Looks like they have been abandoned for a long time.
仙舟有个成语「人走茶凉」，这里就是它的绝佳应用场景。
The Xianzhou has a saying: The tea cools when the person leaves. It's a perfect proverb for this scene.
装饰在柜子上的猫头鹰们盯着你，窃窃私语。
The owls set in the drawer stare at you, whispering among themselves.
这些柜子的密码锁看上去就像猫头鹰的眼睛。你试着拨动码盘，却没有半点反应。
The combination locks on these drawers look just like the eyes of the owls. You try turning the numbered dials, but receive no response.
这屋子的主人想必有许多珍宝要收纳——不然他为何要摆这么多柜子？
The master of this house must have kept many valuables — why would he have so many drawers out otherwise?
桌面被整理得干净又整齐，仿佛一尊供人观赏的标本。
The table is spick and span, like a specimen sample to be admired.
桌上的书籍没有半点灰尘，但你确信它们已经许久不曾被翻动。
There isn't a speck of dust on the books on the table, but you're sure they have not been touched in a long time.
精心划定的一小块空间，具有让人静下心来阅读的魔力。
A small space that has been delicately delineated — it has some alluring charm that calms you down when reading.
被大大小小、奇形怪状的钟表所环绕的角落，或许是「钟表匠」曾经的工作台。
A corner surrounded by clocks big and small, of all strange shapes and sizes. Perhaps it was once the Watchmaker's workbench.
无论这张桌曾经的主人是谁，他肯定不爱整理卫生。
No matter who the master of this table was, he was definitely not a kempt person.
如山的杂物堆积在办公桌表面，皮皮西人甚至很难从中露出自己的脑袋。
Piles of clutter are strewn across the office desk. A Pepeshi person would have found it hard to poke their head through the mess.
扭曲如漩涡的钟表，它所指的时间也混乱如麻。
A watch that swirls like a vortex — the time it tells is also a jumbled chaos.
混沌的时钟，怪诞诡谲一如其所在的混沌梦境。
A chaotic timepiece, strange and absurd, just like the disordered Dreamscape in which it exists.
这怪诞的梦究竟何时才能醒来？钟表和你一样想知道答案。
When will you rouse from this strange dream? Just like you, the clock wishes to know the answer.
你不会从里面找到任何有价值的物品，省省这份力气吧。
You will not find anything of value here. Save your energy.
柜子上的钟表永远指向了某个时刻，你不确定它代表着什么。
The clock on the drawer is permanently pointing at a certain moment, and you're not sure what it means.
梦境是现实印象的投射，而这个梦的主人对柜子的印象并不深刻。
The Dreamscape is a projection of reality's impression, and to this dream's master, the impression of the drawer isn't deep.
空气中满是令人平静的木头气息——不是活的，而是原生的、经过碾压和漂白的木制纸张。
The air is filled with oaken scent that soothes the nerves — not alive, but all-natural, compressed and bleached paper made from wood.
书籍与书架砌成了屋子的墙面，随着视线不断向上延伸。
The books and bookshelf have become the house's walls, rising to heights as far as the eyes see.
这陈腐木板与纸张所铸就的阶梯将通往哪里——愈发混沌的梦境、尚存余温的幻影、还是某段死去已久的回忆？
Where does this staircase made of withered wooden boards and paper go... To an even more chaotic dream, a phantom that lingers with warmth, or some long-dead memory?
即便以梦境的尺度来看，这里的藏书数量也堪称夸张。
Even by Dreamscape measurements, the books stashed here border on hyperbole.
书是人类进步的阶梯——在这里，它的确堆成了一座向上的阶梯。
Books are humanity's staircase to progress. Here, they have literally become a staircase that leads upwards.
书中字迹如同这梦境本身一般，混沌凌乱、无从分辨。
The writing in the books is akin to this Dreamscape itself: Chaotic, disorderly, and incomprehensible.
随手堆砌于此的书籍，也可能是被抛弃了——不知怎地，「抛弃」一词在这个梦中令人格外难受。
The books could have either been casually stacked here, or simply discarded — for some reason, the word "discarded" makes one feel particularly sad in this dream.
富有艺术气息的长桌。从这屋子的布置和藏书来看，主人的品味还算不错。
A long table brimming with artistic flair. From the looks of this house's layout and library, its master had great taste.
过去的某个时刻，这里应该发生了什么事情。可能是一场离别，亦或别的什么催人泪下的场合。
In some past moment, something must have happened here. Perhaps a separation, or some other tragically tearful scene.
你随手从书堆里抽出一本，封面的标题处歪歪扭扭写着：「存在与钟表维修技术」。
You casually pull out a book from a pile, and near the cover title, something is written haphazardly: "Existence and Watch Repair Techniques."
你试着拽了拽这把椅子，纹丝不动。看来它已经被永远定格在了这里。
You pull at this chair, but it doesn't budge. Looks like it's been permanently stuck here.
如果足够有闲心，你甚至可以用这把椅子锻炼肱二头肌。
If you were feeling idle, you could even use this chair to give your biceps a workout.
可惜周围没有梯子，不然你真的很想试试坐上去，悬浮在空中的感觉肯定很棒。
A pity there are no ladders around here, or you might really think of a way to sit in it — being suspended in midair must feel really exciting.
吊灯张开翅膀飞翔，书本在下方笨拙地追赶——光是看着这一幕，你就感到脖子一阵酸痛。
The chandelier spreads its wings and flies, the books clumsily pursuing below — the sheer sight of this is causing a sore pinch in your neck.
这盏灯飞得如此之高，以至于它的打开和关闭都成问题。
This lamp is flying so high that trying to turn it on and off is going to be a problem.
你很好奇，吊灯浮在那个位置究竟是为了照亮什么？
You're curious: What exactly is the chandelier illuminating in that position?
柔软的沙发，仿佛童年最甜美的梦。
A soft sofa akin to the sweetest dreams of childhood.
这座沙发的尺寸可供多人落座，但现在已经空无一人。
This sofa's size can seat multiple people, but it is completely empty now.
如果再多几个孩子，这间屋子或许会变得热闹又温馨。
If there were more children, this house would perhaps be more lively and cozy.
这应当是属于一个孩子的房间，看似温馨，却令人感到不安。
This should have belonged to a child. It looks warm, but fills one with a sense of unease.
积木们零散地堆放着，再无孩子的手将它们拿起。
The blocks are scattered all over, never again picked up by a child's hand.
毫无生气的游乐角，再无孩子的笑声将它填满。
A playtime corner devoid of life, never again filled with the gaiety of a child's laughter.
尚未拆封的盒子，里面藏着孩子所能想象到的最棒礼物。
An unopened box, inside it stashed with all the best gifts that a child could think of.
「不可以打开盒子！」在指尖即将触碰到礼物盒的那个瞬间，一声孱弱的悲鸣在你脑海中炸响，转瞬即逝。
"You can't open this box!" Just as your fingers brush against the present, a frail wail explodes in your mind, and then it is gone.
为庆祝某事而准备的礼物盒，本该属于一场热闹的聚会。
A present prepared for the celebration of a certain event. This should have belonged to a fun party.
无法开启的电视机。漆黑屏幕仿佛濒死者涣散的眼睛，空洞地映出四周明艳的色彩与光影。
A television set that can't be turned on. The pitch-black screen resembles the glazed eyes of a dying person, hollowly reflecting the splendid colors and light around it.
它再也无法放映孩子们喜欢的节目了。
It will never again show the programs that children loved.
电视上拴着两个无精打采的气球，可能是某次聚会的残余物。
Two listless balloons are secured to the television set — possible remnants of some party.
被气球吊死的钢琴。偶尔还会发出几声沙哑的、不和谐的颤音。
A piano lynched by balloons, occasionally emitting raspy, discordant trills.
你很好奇，悬在空中的钢琴是否能发出和平时相同的声响，但似乎没有机会尝试了。
You're curious: Can a piano suspended in midair make the sounds it usually makes? But it seems you won't have a chance to try it out.
一台价值不菲的钢琴，看来住在这间屋里的孩子受到了良好的音乐教育。
A very expensive piano. It seems that the child of this house received excellent music education.
没有阳光的梦里，只有假花才能茁壮生长。
In the sunless dream, only fake flowers are allowed to grow.
这抹为惨淡光线所浸透的翠绿，并未能带来任何生机，反倒平添几许寒意。
This emerald green soaked in bleak light does not bring about life, but rather adds a shroud of chill instead.
架子上摆着许多书本，内容大部分是天马行空的童话。
Many books are on display on the shelf, in which most of them are written fantastical fairy tales.
架上的书籍没有半点灰尘，但你确信它们已经许久不曾被翻动。
There isn't a speck of dust on the books on the shelf, but you're sure they have not been touched in a long time.
有棱有角的雕像，按照孩童的喜好进行了设计，似乎是折纸小鸟的原型。
A statue with edges and corners, folded according to a child's design. Looks like the shape of an origami bird.
威武的鸟类雕像，或许是梦境的某种象征。
A majestic statue of a bird. Perhaps it's a symbol of the Dreamscape.
坚固的箱子保护着稚子最为重要的秘宝。
A sturdy chest that protects a child's most important treasure.
9-<unbreak>15</unbreak>-7…保险箱旁不知被谁刻了这样一组数字，你试了试，金属锁扣岿然不动。
9-<unbreak>15</unbreak>-7... Someone carved these numbers into the side of the safe. You try the combination, but the metal lock still won't budge.
「被遗弃…我们…出不去…」细小的嘈杂声，从大脑深处窸窸窣窣地钻出，敲击在脆弱的鼓膜上，「留下来吧…这里太空了…留下吧……」
"Abandoned... We... We can't get out..." A soft cacophony, rustling from deep within your mind and knocking on your fragile eardrums. "Stay with us... It's too lonely here... Stay..."
已失去使用价值的电视，如抱团取暖般堆砌着，仿佛要对抗孤独带来的寒冷。
Televisions that have lost their value. They are all piled up, as if huddling together to fend off the chills from loneliness.
你盯着成堆的电视，感觉无数只眼睛也在同时盯着你。
You stare at the pile of televisions, and feel like there are countless eyes staring at you at the same time.
水幕如帘，波光映亮了墙壁。鲸群自远方巡游，像是要冲入头顶的另一片天地。
A cascading curtain, where a glistening light of waves illuminates the walls. A pod of whales is swimming in the distance, as if trying to break through the ceiling of the sky.
一切都颠倒了过来：瀑布逆流而上，鲸群奔向梦境的裂口，仿佛要坠入深渊，又像是在追逐光明。
Everything is upside down: Waterfalls flow upwards, the pod of whales rushing towards the breach in the Dreamscape, as if trying to dive into the abyss, but at the same time chasing after the light.
这架望远镜内播放着苏乐达的系列广告，而且不断循环，似乎永无休止。
The telescope displays an endless loop of SoulGlad commercials.
这架望远镜透出了正对面的苏乐达工厂大楼，清晰无比——总算有个能用的望远镜了。
This telescope is functional and provides a clear view into the SoulGlad factory building.
只是…它似乎没有放大功能。你左看右看，觉得还是直接用双眼欣赏这副景象更好。
However... it lacks magnification. Enjoying the view with your naked eyes seems preferable.
「您正在呼叫的是，『蓝调的时刻』。愿您在梦泡之海的舞会上被浪漫垂青。」
"You are calling the Blue Hour. May you find romantic encounters at the ball in the Ocean of Dream Bubbles."
「您正在呼叫的是，『薄暮的时刻』。愿您在匹诺康尼拍卖会中收获心仪的宝物。」
"You are calling the Moment of Dusk. May you find your desired treasures at the Penacony Auction Hall."
「您正在呼叫的是，『星辰的时刻』。愿您在机动球大赛中旗开得胜、一车当先。」
"You are calling the Moment of Stars. May you emerge victorious in the Roboball Contest."
「您正在呼叫的是，『太阳的时刻』。『初醒图书馆』将随时对您敞开大门。」
"You are calling the Moment of Sol. The gates to the Primal Waking Library are always open to you."
「您正在呼叫的是，『绿洲的时刻』。愿您在日光公园的午后尽享安恬。」
"You are calling the Moment of Oasis. Please enjoy a peaceful afternoon at Sunshine Park."
<color=#eb4d3dff>「不要打给『安谧的时刻』！重复一遍，不要打给『安谧的时刻』！那里不对外开放！」</color>
<color=#eb4d3dff>"Don't call the Moment of Serenity! I repeat, do not call the Moment of Serenity! It's off-limits!"</color>
你敲了敲三月七的房门，里面毫无反应，可能她这会儿正在某个梦境时刻里四处拍照呢。
You knock on the door to March 7th's room, but no one responds. She's probably busy snapping photos in one of the Dreamscape moments.
属于熟悉女孩子的房间，不过这次没有对你敞开大门。
The room belongs to a girl with whom you are close, but the door isn't open to you this time.
一股浓郁的咖啡味从屋内传来…这可不方便入梦啊。
A rich aroma of coffee wafts from within the room... It seems to be a bad time for a dream.
你原打算敲敲姬子的房间门，但终究没有忍心去打扰她。
You planned to knock on the door to Himeko's room but decided not to bother her in the end.
你试着拽了拽门把手——纹丝不动，看来杨叔的安全意识很高。
You give the knob a twist, but the door doesn't budge. Looks like Mr. Yang has a high safety awareness.
瓦尔特的房间，散发着一股莫名「沉重」的气息，足以吓退那些不怀好意的宾客。
Welt's room gives off a somewhat somber aura, which is enough to intimidate guests who are up to no good.
一扇紧锁的房门，门后传来浓郁的香气，不知是来自花朵还是高档香水…或许你需要某些凭证才能将它打开。

A thick and heady fragrance drifts from the space behind the firmly locked door. You wonder whether it is the scent of flowers or a premium perfume... Perhaps, you need some sort of permit to unlock the door.
一扇紧锁的房门，门后传来轻微的电流声。直觉告诉你，强行打开这扇门可能会有坏事发生。
A subtle buzzing sound of electricity comes from the space behind the firmly locked door. Your instinct tells you that opening the door by force might lead to unthinkable consequences.
科琳的房间。除非她想出来，不然没人进得去——你已经知道那什么「锁闭式次元力场发生器」的存在了。
Colleen's room. No one can open the door unless she opens it herself. You are aware of the enclosed dimensional force field generator on the door.
一扇紧锁的房门。屋内没有传来任何声音，紧锁的门又在暗示它确实有人居住。
A firmly locked door. The room is quiet, but the fact that the door is locked indicates that it is occupied.
查德威克的房间，也是他人生中曾经历过的众多监牢之一。
Chadwick's room. It is also one of the many prisons he's lived in his lifetime.
这扇房门不会再度开启了——它已经封存了太多东西。
This door will never be opened again. Too many things are sealed in this room.
一扇紧锁的房门，门后传来淡淡的灰尘气息，似乎已经很久没有打扫——这并不奇怪，总有人不愿从梦中醒来。

A faint smell of dust comes from the space behind the firmly locked door, suggesting that the room has not been cleaned for quite some time. This is not unusual, as there are people who refuse to wake up from the dream.
正在打扫中的房间，很快就会迎来下一位追逐美梦的住客。
Cleaning is in progress. The room will soon welcome a new guest.
视野绝佳的观景台，几乎能将整个筑梦边境的景色尽收眼底。
The observation deck with breathtaking views. You can almost see the entire Dream's Edge at a glance.
这确实是个不容易发现的地方。向你介绍这里的人一定非常珍视你们的关系、才会把这幅美景与你分享。
It's not an easy place to find. The person who introduced it to you truly values your friendship by sharing this view.
你回想起和那个女孩共度的时光，她柔软的笑容，她轻盈的步伐，以及她最后一刻、被那怪物斩杀时流下的泪。
You recall the times spent with that girl. Her gentle smile, her graceful steps, and the tears she shed in her last moments before she was murdered by that monster.
那个时候，她为什么要说「对不起」呢？她脑海中所想的会是什么呢？倘若你们动作再快一点的话，那傻丫头是不是就不用死了呢？
At that moment, why did she apologize? What was she thinking? If you had reacted faster, would that silly girl not have died?
一股酸涩从你心底油然而生，随后是愤怒的火焰——足以将最坚固的金属烧成灰烬。
A tinge of sourness wells from the depths of your heart, followed by a flame of anger — intense enough to reduce the strongest metal to ashes.
面朝栏杆与高楼，你握紧了拳头……
Facing the railings and skyscrapers, you clench your fists...
适合潜入、隐藏；不宜追逐战。
Suitable for infiltration and concealment; not suitable for pursuit.
如果说下水道是一座城市的地底迷宫，那么通风管道便是高居穹顶的幽邃秘境。
If the sewers are a city's underground labyrinth, then ventilation ducts are the dome's well-kept secret.
糖浆味的烟雾从通风口里飘出…看来梦里的排气也是香的。
Syrupy smoke wafts through the vents... Seems like the exhaust in dreams smells good too.
你朝管道里丢了块石子，许久都没有回声传来。看来从这里爬进去的计划不太现实。
You throw a stone into the pipe, but you can't hear the echo. It's not a realistic plan to crawl through the pipe.
高楼的换气装置，由于造型奇特，筑梦师们都叫它「调料罐」。
The Dreamweavers call the ventilators in the skyscrapers "seasoning boxes" due to their peculiar shape.
「无动力通风球」，在任何情况下都能保证建筑内外的空气流通。
The non-kinetic ventilation ball ensures air circulation within and around the building under all circumstances.
——请不要将手指伸进叶片的缝隙间，除非你想被强制送回现实酒店。
Please do not stick your fingers between the slits in the blades, unless you wish to be forced to return to the hotel in reality.
探照灯，顾名思义，以强大的光源在黑暗中探索照明——但这玩意在灯火不熄的城市中能有多大用处呢？
The searchlight, as its name implies, offers a powerful light source for exploration in the dark. Yet, how beneficial are they in a city with constant illumination?
这些探照灯最大的用处，就是测试新梦境的光线追踪是否有效。
The greatest use for these searchlights is to test the effectiveness of the new Dreamscape's ray tracing.
都市传说一则：在有些夜晚，这盏灯会在天空中投射出巨大的有翼哺乳动物剪影——这说明有一位暗夜英雄即将出手。
According to urban legend, on certain nights, this lamp casts the silhouette of a giant winged mammal in the sky — a signal that a superhero of the night is poised to strike.
这盏探照灯的外壳破旧，光线昏黄而苍凉，你很好奇它究竟这样守望了多久。
This searchlight's shell is old and worn, and its light is dim and weak. You wonder how long it's been standing watch.
此处宜人的环境适合短暂的休憩，或者摸鱼。
The serene surroundings are perfect for a short break, or to slack off.
「禁止踩踏灌木、攀爬喷泉！」
"No treading on shrubs. No climbing on fountains!"
注：物品仅供演示，最终效果请以实际梦境为准。
Note: Items are for demonstration purposes. For the final results, please refer to the Dreamscape.
「浇灌用水，不可直接饮用。」
"Irrigation water, not for consumption."
「请勿靠近，以防跌落。」
"Keep a safe distance to prevent potential falls."
这里的天窗很高，如果不小心掉下去，或许在现实中醒来时也得头痛好一会。
The skylight here is so high that if you were to accidentally fall in, you might wake up in reality with a headache.
温暖的光透过玻璃传来，里面似乎是为舞会准备的布景，但现在空无一人。
Warm rays of light penetrate in through the glass. A seeming stage is prepared for a dance performance, except there's naught a soul there.
「不要幻想从这里跳下去，里面的场景都还没做完。」来自某位筑梦师的留言。
"Don't harbor delusions of jumping down there. The set pieces aren't done yet." A note from a Dreamweaver.
一般而言，这种水塔里既不会有泡发的尸体，也不存在美人鱼。
Usually speaking, such water towers won't have corpses soaked in them, nor will they house mermaids.
不必怀疑，这玩意虽然叫做「水塔」，里面装的也都是苏乐达。
There's no need to inquire: This thing may be called a water tower, but it's all SoulGlad stored in here.
很难想象，曾经竟有不止一名游客试图在这块广告牌顶部蹦极。
It's hard to imagine that more than one tourist has bungee jumped off the top of this billboard.
「广告位招租，价高者优先。」
"Billboard space for rent. Highest bidders first."
等新梦境开放后，人们就会为这面广告牌争相竞价。
When the new Dreamscape goes public, many will clamor to bid for this billboard space.
集成式能源站，为整个筑梦边境的灯光提供支持。等地图正式开放后，这种建筑的数量会翻上几番。
An integrated power station that supports all the lights of the entire Dream's Edge. When the map goes public, such structures will surely exponentially grow in number.
为筑梦边境提供能源的小屋，总有人对它的原理感到好奇——最后他们要么打不开门，要么只能发现里面空无一物。

A little house that provides power for the Dream's Edge. There'll always be some who are curious about the principles behind its operation. Eventually, either they can't get the door open, or they'll find that there's nothing behind it.
家族监管局，负责监督整个筑梦边境的工程进展——顺带驱逐那些不该出现的家伙。
The Family's surveillance agency, charged with overseeing the entire Dream's Edge's construction, as well as driving out people who should not be there.
再狂野的想象也需要制约，这便是家族监管局的职责。
Even the wildest of imaginations must be curbed — that is the duty of The Family's surveillance agency.
为筑梦师们打造的休憩场所。经常有人在这里光明正大地摸鱼。
A lounge center built for the Dreamweavers. There are people openly playing hooky in here.
筑梦师们的脾气往往和名气成正比，所以当他们试图在某个问题上达成一致时，不论动口还是动手都不稀奇。
The tempers and renown of Dreamweavers are typically proportionate, which is why, when they try to come to consensus on a certain problem, it's not uncommon to see verbal debates turn to blows.
脑力工作需要更多的休息时间，这间酒吧就是专门为此而建。
Work of the mind requires even more rest, which is what this bar was specifically designed for.
门扉紧锁——这里似乎并不欢迎访客。
The door is tightly locked — this place seemingly does not welcome visitors.
你推了推，大门纹丝不动；又推了推——还是别浪费力气啦！
You push on it, but the door does not budge. You try again... Best not to waste your strength!
门锁着，这算是一种保护——毕竟没人能说清门后的空间有没有建造完毕。
The door is locked, which is a kind of protection in itself — no one is really clear on whether or not the construction behind the door is complete.
这是一架看起来能爬的梯子——你试了一下，发现它压根没有物理碰撞。
This looks like a ladder that can be scaled. You give it a shot, and notice that it has absolutely no physics collision.
梯子连接的建筑顶端还没有建造完成，任何踩上去的人都会坠落——或许这就是梯子不能爬的原因。
The roof of the building that the ladder is attached to is incomplete. Anyone who steps on it will fall... Perhaps this is the reason why the ladder can't be scaled.
某种预感告诉你，哪怕是梦境建成之后，这架梯子也不能爬。
A hunch tells you that even after this Dreamscape is built, you won't be able to climb this ladder.
视野绝佳的观景台，几乎能将整个筑梦边境的景色尽收眼底。
This observation deck has breathtaking views. You can almost see the entire Dream's Edge at a glance.
这确实是个不容易发现的地方。向你介绍这里的人一定非常珍视你们的关系、才会把这幅美景与你分享。
It's not an easy place to find. The person who introduced it to you truly values your friendship by sharing this view.
你与那女孩创造的回忆已经不胜枚举。无论快乐与否，它们都会像吃进肚里的橡木蛋糕卷一样，变成你身体的一部分。
The memories you created with that girl are too many to count. Whether they were happy or otherwise, they will become a part of you, like an Oak Cake Roll eaten and ending up in your belly.
先前的嘈杂情绪渐渐消失了，取而代之的是一抹平静，仿佛没有流星划过的、现实的夜空。
The previously mixed emotions have gradually disappeared, replaced by a palette of serenity, a night sky of reality unstreaked by shooting stars.
你明白，你和她都有要完成的使命，或在梦境、或在现实。为此你们不得不暂时分离，走向命运为各自安排的道路。
You know that both of you have a mission to complete, be it in the Dreamscape or reality. To this end, you two are forced to go separate paths, toward your own pre-ordained destinies.
等一切结束后，你们还会再见的——你如此相信着。希望那时她一切安好。
When all of this is over, you will see each other again — that is what you believe. You hope she will be safe and well then.
视野绝佳的观景台，几乎能将整个筑梦边境的景色尽收眼底。
The observation deck with breathtaking views. You can almost see the entire Dream's Edge at a glance.
这确实是个不容易发现的地方。向你介绍这里的人一定非常珍视你们的关系、才会把这幅美景与你分享。
It's not an easy place to find. The person who introduced it to you truly values your friendship by sharing this view.
就像每本书都有最后一页，你在匹诺康尼的故事也迎来了落幕。于结尾前的高潮部分，那女孩化作了天上的流星。
Just as every book has its final page, your story in Penacony has come to an end. In the climactic moments leading up to the conclusion, the girl became a shooting star in the sky.
如今，「开拓」的道路再次分散出千百种可能性，是时候重新踏上旅途了。无论你选择去往何处，楼顶的这片天空都将永远映在你的梦中。

Now, the Path of Trailblaze is once again brimming with countless possibilities. It is time to embark on a new journey. No matter where you're headed, the sky above the building will always be etched in your dreams.
这景色，还挺漂亮的…你知道，它不是虚假的幻影。
You know, this breathtaking view in front of your eyes... is definitely not an illusion.
毕竟，栏杆尽头的远方是一片黎明。
After all, there is a dawn waiting at the end of the railing, visible in the distance.
茶几上的茶具们开始微微颤动…它们仿佛有话要说。
The tea utensils on the tea set start to tremble... as if they have something to say.
茶杯
Teacup
「我们得从这里离开！■■就要打进来了！」
"We have to get out of here! ■■ is about to breach this place!"
茶壶
Teapot
「混蛋，不要散布紧张情绪！我们有人，也有武器，可以应付……」
"Fool, stop spreading anxiety! We've got men and weapons. We'll manage..."
茶杯
Teacup
「就凭我们，你疯了吗？！■■会把我们撕成碎片！」
"With just us? Are you crazy!? ■■ will tear us to shreds!"
茶壶
Teapot
「没什么…这没什么，我们可是亡命之徒，记得吗？现在给我坐下，喝了这杯…我们应付得来，我们绝对能挺过去……」
"It's nothing... This is nothing. We're desperados on the lam, remember? Now sit down and drink this... We'll manage, and we'll definitely overcome this..."
你被眼前的奇景所吸引，攫取了全部心神；
You're mesmerized by the wondrous sight before you — it robs you of your full attention.
沉重脚步拖拽着你前进，就像是夜蛾赴火；
Heavy footsteps drag toward you up ahead, like moths to a flame.
灵魂正催促你抛弃苦果，跨过这无形之门；
Souls urge you to unburden your woes and step through this formless door.
而后你的一切去伪存真，与古老之梦共存。
Where you will shed your falsities and embrace the truth, co-existing with the ancient dream.
只可惜——哦不，应该说可喜可贺——你根本过不去。
Alas... Or rather, congratulations — you can't go through at all.
「通过我，将进入混沌之梦」
"Step past me and enter the Dream of Chaos"
「通过我，将进入亘古之虚空」
"Step past me and enter the Immemorial Void"
「通过我，将进入漆黑之活塚」
"Step past me and enter the Living Grave of Dark"
「在我之先，无物实存，唯不朽者不朽」
"Before me, there was nothing, apart from the immortal"
「于我之下，万物流转，方嬗变者嬗变」
"After me, everything flowed, when all evolved"
你望向「尽头」，那里似乎有什么无法窥见的存在，正于永恒静默之中，吟唱着不可言说的赞美诗。
You gaze at the "End" — there appears to be some unseen presence there, singing an unspeakable hymn in the ever-silence.
架子鼓正在呜咽，你知道这种乐器在现实中绝不可能发出近似的声音。
The drum kit whimpers — you know that this instrument would never make a sound remotely close to this in reality.
架子鼓
Drum Kit
「…我们不会抛下他…我们会在一起…一直一直……」
"...We won't abandon him... We will... always, always... together..."
小号
Trumpet
「哭吧！尽情哭吧！■■可不会放过我们——我们都是犯过错的罪人！我们是犯过错的罪人！」
"Cry! Cry out all you want! ■■ will never let us off! We are sinners who have sinned! We are sinners who have sinned!"
架子鼓
Drum Kit
「谁来救救他…谁来救救我们……」
"Who will save him... Who will save us..."
小号
Trumpet
「谁也不会来！谁也不会来！罪人不配拥有救星，只配被■■永远奴役！」
"No one will come! No one will come! Sinners do not deserve saviors, and they only deserve to be enslaved by ■■!"
在你靠近的一瞬，台子上的锅碗瓢盆通通躁动起来。恍惚间，你似乎听到了模糊的人声……
The moment you get near, the pots and pans on the counter move about restlessly. Suddenly, you seem to hear a muffled human voice...
嗝…来喝一杯吧，苏乐达…它真的很好喝！
*gulp*... Come have a drink, SoulGlad... It's really tasty!
狱警
Warden
现在？你怕不是在开玩笑吧？
Now? You must be kidding me, right?
狱警
Warden
等一下…我记得你！之前在大厅里，那群囚犯都围着你欢呼，难道你就是他们的头头？
Hold on... I remember you! The surrounding convicts were cheering you on at the lobby. Could you be their leader?
…今天是个快乐的日子，我先…敬你们一杯。朋友们，为了健康！
...Today's a joyous day. Let me first... raise my glass to you all. To health, my friends!
狱警
Warden
快乐的日子？呵，对你们来说或许是，不过……
A joyous day? Heh, it might be to you, but...
一声枪响，随后是重物落地的声音。似乎是怕那囚犯还有口气，狱警又接着补了几枪。
A gunshot rings out, followed by a heavy thud. Probably afraid that the prisoner is still breathing, the warden adds a few more shots to finish the job.
狱警
Warden
这群囚犯天天喝那什么甜汽水，把脑子都喝傻了…喂，你们几个，过来把地面清理干净！
What's that sweet soda these convicts are drinking all day? It's rotting their brains... Hey, you lot, clean up this floor!
回过神来时，你感觉怒火已经填满了胸膛。
When you come to your senses, you feel like rage is exploding from your chest.
触碰到这簇忆泡时，一股浓郁的、莫名的情绪淹没了你。
When you touch this memory bubble cluster, an intense, inexplicable emotion drowns you.
破碎的画面一闪而过。你看到了离别，听到了哭声，尝到了嘴角咸咸的液体。
Fragments of images flash by. You see parting, hear crying, and taste salty liquid in the corner of your mouth.
当你从这情绪中回过神来时，一滴泪已经流到了耳边……
When you recover from this emotion, a single tear has reached the side of your ear...
喷泉中汩汩流淌的梦泡对你说着悄悄话：「真实与存在都是伪命题，快跳进来吧——和我们、和这个梦融为一体！」
The dream bubble blubbering from the fountain whispers to you: "Truth and existence are false propositions. Hurry up and jump in — become one with us in this dream!"
靠近这座喷泉时，你感觉到一阵恍惚。巨大的梦泡仿佛长出了眼睛和双手……
When you move near this fountain, you feel a wave of stupor. A giant dream bubble appears to have grown eyes and hands...
「逐梦客，把我喝掉！」 它指向自己饱满的身躯，热情地发出邀请……
"Dreamchaser, drink me!" It points to its own bubbling body, warmly extending an invitation...
头顶那巨型茶杯的大眼睛不再滴溜溜乱转——此刻它正紧紧盯着你。
The huge eyes atop the giant teacup stop darting around profusely — it is now fervently staring at you.
「喝了它，这是逐梦客的命运！」它指着喷泉里的梦泡，聒噪地在你脑子里大喊大叫。
"Drink it. This is the destiny of a Dreamchaser!" It points to the dream bubble in the fountain, noisily yelling in your head.
可…代价是什么呢？
But... What is the price?
平平无奇的储物架。上面没有子弹、火柴、草药…洗手液或是灌装凝胶。
An ordinary storage shelf. There are no bullets, matches, herbs on it... nor are there hand sanitizers or filling gel.
恍惚中那蓝色蝴蝶向你飞来，翅膀挥动间洒下艳丽却致命的鳞片。
The azure butterfly flits towards you, its wings scattering splendid, fatal scales.
你想要尖叫、想要哀嚎，声音却随着血液与以太一同冻结，等待你的唯有静谧的死亡与深不见底的绝望……
You want to scream, to howl, but your voice is frozen with blood and ether, awaiting your serene death and unfathomable despair...
然而片刻之后，你清醒过来，长舒一口气——相框中安静地匍匐着一只死去已久的普通蝴蝶，而你也并非什么黑魔法师。
And then in an instant, you awaken, inhaling a long drag of air — in the picture frame, an ordinary long-dead butterfly sleeps, and you're no magician of the dark arts.
人们说书是记忆的有形载体。但在充斥着梦泡的酒店中，这一墙书本就显得相形见绌了。
People say books are the physical medium of memories, but in the hotel filled with dream bubbles, this wall of books looks puny in comparison.
在名为「匹诺康尼」的梦中，被梦见的人醒了。
In the dream called "Penacony," the person who was being dreamed about rouses.
你曾在此醒来。如今你感到好奇，倘若于这破碎梦中就此睡去，再次睁开眼时自己将出现在哪里？
You once woke up here. Now you are curious: If you sleep here in this fragmented dream, where would you appear when you once again open your eyes?
形似贝壳的深眠装置，质感奢华。躺进其中之后，催眠气体便会包裹你的全身。
A shell-shaped deep-sleep device, luxuriously crafted. When you lie down inside, hypnotic gases will envelop your body.
皮革沙发。光滑、柔软，香橙果冻一般Q弹。
A leather sofa — glossy and sleek, soft, and bouncy like orange jelly.
舒适的沙发。适合在上面尽情舒展、来回翻滚，就好似小丑鱼在艳丽珊瑚丛中嬉戏——只可惜你根本坐不下去。
A comfy sofa. Perfect for stretching out, or rolling about, like a clownfish playfully weaving about colorful coral. A pity you can't even sit on it.
杯中空无一物，不知何日才能再度填满。
The cup is empty, not knowing which day it will be full again.
闲置的茶具整齐摆在桌面上，曾经的欢谈情景仿佛还历历在目。
The idle tea set is neatly arranged on the table, conversations and sights of past still vivid in one's mind.
空荡荡的前台，像现实里那样门庭若市的情景怕是再也看不见了。
An empty front desk — bustling crowds of reality will probably not be seen.
这里是家族的安全区，可以抵御忆域迷因的袭击。
This is The Family's safe zone that can fend off Memory Zone Meme attacks.
即便不再有人需要办理入住，家族依旧妥善保养着梦中的柜台，以免某天需要将它用作最后的防线。
Even if there's no one else to check in, The Family still maintains the reception in the dream, in case it needs to be used as the last line of defense one day.
酒店公用的梦境电话，可以打给不同「时刻」…不知它还能不能用了。
The hotel's public dreamphone that can call different Moments... Is it still operational?
你拿起听筒，里面只传来无意义的尖鸣声。
You pick up the receiver, and all you can hear is meaningless static.
破碎的梦境失了惯常的引力，就连循规蹈矩的行李都开始变得放荡不羁。
The fragmented dream has lost its usual allure, and even the orderly baggage has begun to stray awry.
「来跳舞吗？」黄金的行李架提议，「和我们一起、和头顶的鲸群一起……」
"Dance?" A golden suitcase proposes, "With us, with the pod of whales above..."
行李飞向天空，如同骏鹰挣脱枷锁。
The suitcase flies into the sky, like a hawk breaking free of its fetters.
「嘿，你知道吗！」古怪的屏幕兴致勃勃地叫住了路过的你，「从现在起，我『免费』啦！」
"Hey, did you know!" The odd screen excitedly stops you as you pass, "From now on, I'm free!"
梦泡如虫茧般附在这面屏幕上，使其肮脏如污泥。
Dream bubbles are plastered on this screen like insect cocoons, making it dirty as sludge.
屏幕上显示出一些不太「常规」的网站入口，你不会想要点进去的。
A few strange web interfaces are shown on the screen. You don't really want to click into them.
梦泡如虫茧般附在这面屏幕上，使之模糊如锈铁。
Dream bubbles are plastered on this screen like insect cocoons, making it blurry as rusted metal.
球笼服务已暂时停运，恢复日期尚未确定。
The spheroid service has been halted indefinitely.
这种时候还有谁会想要使用这些载具呢——惊梦剧团、还是忆域迷因？
Who would even use these vehicles at a time like this — the Dreamjolt Troupe, or the Memory Zone Memes?
被污染的梦境入口，如同一只从深渊中睁开的眼睛。
The tainted Dreamscape entrance, akin to an open eye in the abyss.
「不要靠近那圈光环，否则你将失去自我。」一个声音在你耳边低语道。
"Don't get close to that light circle, or you'll lose yourself." A low voice rumbles beside your ear.
几个梦泡占据了沙发前的空间，这些座位现在已经被禁止使用…不过，或许可以试试？
A few dream bubbles occupy the space in front of the sofa. These seats are now prohibited from usage... But, why not give it a whirl?
长沙发最适合睡觉了，尤其是你还可以拿那些梦泡当枕头。
The sofa is most appropriate for sleeping, especially when you can use these dream bubbles as pillows.
即使梦境的一滴水珠，落在座位上也是一场湍流。
Even a single water drop in the Dreamscape becomes a deluge when dropped on the seat.
梦泡带来的紊乱侵扰了这处座位，现在它已经不适合被任何人使用。
The disorder brought about by the dream bubbles have infested this seat — it is now unsuitable for usage.
人们说书是记忆的有形载体。但在充斥着梦泡的酒店中，这一墙书本就显得相形见绌了。
People say books are the physical medium of memories, but in the hotel filled with dream bubbles, this wall of books looks puny in comparison.
「看完记得放回原位！」当你触碰到某本书时，书的合页突然说出这么句话，吓得你赶紧抽回了手。
"Don't forget to put them back in the right spots when you're done!" When you touch a book, its spine suddenly blurts out a reminder, causing you to pull your hand back in shock.
曾经完整的圆桌，如今已四分五裂。
A once complete round table, now shattered into pieces.
或许从前有一大家人围在这条桌前共进晚餐，但现在他们和桌子都不再完整了。
Maybe there was a big family who ate dinner at this table, but now both they and the table are incomplete.
「…为什么…不嗨起来……」说罢，架子鼓发出了几声闷响，声音如同铁锤敲击墙壁。
"... Why... can't we get high" On that note, the drum set produces a few muffled notes, like a hammer knocking away at a wall.
「啦啦啦…噜噜噜…啦啦啦噜！」小号的声音如同垂死女人的尖叫。
"Lalala... Doodoodoo... Lalaladoo!" A trumpet's sound screams like a dying woman.
架子鼓不自然地抖动着，时不时传来低沉的吼声，仿佛有一只怪物正蒙在鼓里。
The drum kit unnaturally starts to shake, intermittently emitting low howling sounds, like a monster sealed in it.
「…到…最后…他也没有…离开…我们不会离开……」
"... Till... the last moment... he didn't... leave... We won't leave..."
一道欢快的嗓音在这空荡荡的宴会厅中炸开、回响：「诸位的掌声与喝彩是对我们最大的鼓励，谢谢大家——」
A cheerful voice explodes and reverberates through the empty banquet hall: "Your applause and cheers are the greatest motivation for us. Thank you, everyone..."
恍惚间你仿佛听见提琴低沉优雅的嗓音：「我们的演奏…您觉得好听…吗…？」
You seem to hear a low, elegant violin: "Did you like... our performance...?"
「好听吗好听吗好听吗好听吗好听吗好听吗好听吗好听吗好听吗好听吗好听吗好听吗好听吗好听吗好听吗好好好好好好好好好听吗——」
"Didyoulikedidyoulikedidyoulikedidyoulikedidyoulikedidyoulikedidyoulikedidyoulikedidyoulike—"
据说在梦境酒店还正常时，这个房间压根无需侍者、食物自己就能端上顾客的桌。
It's said that when the Hotel in the dreamscape was running, this room had no need for attendants — food would serve themselves on guests' tables.
相比于盐和胡椒，梦中最不可或缺的调料是爱。
Rather than salt and pepper, love is the key ingredient in a dream.
柜门后是梦境最为隐蔽的空间，非常适合躲藏——但时间久了还是会被忆域迷因抓出来。
Behind the cabinet door is the Dreamscape's most hidden space, very good for hiding. But even after a while, one would still be caught and dragged away by the Memory Zone Memes.
紧锁的柜门，它背后的秘密永远无法为人所知了。
A tightly-shut cabinet door, the secrets behind it forever locked away from anyone's knowledge.
全垒打，幸好没人站在下面。
A home run — thankfully no one was standing below.
吊灯深深嵌进了地板里，它的重击足以让人从梦中醒来十次。
The chandelier is deeply lodged into the floor, its heavy impact enough to awaken one from a dream ten times over.
屏幕上只余无意义的画面，伴随着刺耳的电流声。
Only garbled visuals are left on the screen, accompanied by the sounds of an ear-piercing electrical current.
荧幕投出的冷光让房间显得更为孤寂，可你怎么也关不上它。
The cold light projected from the screen showers the room with even more loneliness. You can't turn it off no matter what you do.
如果有什么东西想要从这堆屏幕里爬出来的话，它该不会迷路吧？
If something wanted to crawl out from those screens, it wouldn't get lost, would it?
当你望向这些屏幕的时候，它们背后或许有什么也在回望着你。
When you gaze at these screens, perhaps something behind them is gazing back at you.
这些屏幕上只有无意义的画面，伴随着刺耳的电流声。
Only garbled visuals are left on these screens, accompanied by the sounds of an ear-piercing electrical current.
记载于纸上的资料，效率比梦泡更低，但更加可靠。
Information recorded on paper may be less efficient than dream bubbles, but is far more reliable.
可以预见整理这些纸张会是件多么困难的事。
Tidying up these papers would be such a hassle.
巨大的梦泡淹没了这座电梯。倘若你不想被混乱的记忆填满，就离它远一点吧。
Giant dream bubbles have flooded this elevator. If you don't want to be drowning in chaotic memories, you'd better stay away from the elevator.
由于忆质的阻隔，酒店各层已经失去了联系，电梯自然也无法使用了。
Memoria isolation has caused all hotel floors to lose comms capability, which also means the elevator is naturally out of order.
梦泡，凝结的梦境精华。
Dream bubbles, made up of condensed Dreamscape essence.
是谁从梦境之河里舀出了它们？
Who scooped them up from the Dreamscape's River?
每团梦泡里的美梦都足以填满人的脑海——当然，噩梦也是。
Every dream in the dream bubbles is enough to fill a person's head — as can the nightmares.
指尖大小的一点便蕴含着人的一生。
Something the size of a fingertip is enough to contain a life's worth of experiences.
舒适的皮革沙发。可惜这里缺少能让它实现存在价值的对象。
A comfortable leather sofa. Unfortunately, it lacks an object that would help it realize its worth.
台前已无来宾，唯余座椅冰凉。
There are no guests at the front desk, and the seats have turned cold.
「干杯！」酒杯们在空中彼此相碰，假装出这里还有人时的热闹景象。
"Cheers!" The wine glasses are clinking in the air, pretending that there is still a bustling scene filled with people.
你看着雅座上空不安分的伞棚，不由想起每当台风天气出行，你与伞是如何艰难地领受大自然的馈赠。
You look at the canopy above the private seats and can't help but be reminded of travel during typhoon season — how you and your umbrella braved the ardors of nature's gift.
不知道从哪飘来的伞座，它的存在属实有些突兀，更恼人的是，你还搬不动它。
Not sure where the umbrella seat came from, its appearance is quite a surprise. What's more annoying is that you can't move it.
这才叫宴会——足够高调、足够热闹！而且…足够「嗨」！
This is what I call a party! It's high profile and lively enough, and it's definitely "high" enough!
「该死的重力！」飞得最低的那把椅子说。
"Darn gravity!" says the chair that's flying the lowest.
你尝试着打了一杆。啪——黑球应声入洞。看来你此前苦练出的技术还未生疏。
You try your hand at a bat. Whack! The black ball goes into the hole. Looks like you haven't lost your hard-earned skills.
你继续出杆。哎呀…糟糕，红球跑偏了。你想要一杆清台的愿望就此破灭。
You continue to bat. Oh dear... Oh no, the red ball went astray. Your hopes of a clean shot are dashed.
你继续出杆——反正没人对局，想怎么打是你的自由。但这次力道似乎大了些，蓝球撞到了天花板上。你很疑惑，先前从未发现自己的臂力是如此地惊人。

You continue to bat, since no one's playing, and you're free to hit however you want. But this time you use too much force, causing the blue ball to hit the ceiling. You're puzzled — you never noticed you had such a strong arm before.
你继续出杆。可这次…黄球仿佛发了疯似的在房间里飞舞起来！它的运动轨迹毫无规律，速度也快得惊人，最后径直砸进了你的上衣口袋里。
You continue to bat. But this time... The yellow ball goes nuts and starts dancing around the room! It's moving erratically at shocking speeds, and it ends up in your shirt pocket.
…在打出第五杆前，你最好先祈祷物理学在梦境里也存在。
...Before you bat your fifth shot, you better pray that physics exist in the Dreamscape.
你在梦中，看见时间的轮盘变形融化，嘀嗒、嘀嗒——
In your dream, you witness the wheels of time morphing and melting, tick-tock, tick-tock.
扭曲的钟表，它所指的时刻和梦境本身一样混乱。
Twisted clocks, the moments they point to are as confusing as the Dreamscape.
倘若这是一个飞镖盘，那么它的上靶率肯定低到发指。
If this were a dartboard, it would have a ridiculously low hit rate.
欢迎光临「惊梦酒吧」。店主近期不在，营业时间另行通知。有事请留言。
Welcome to Dreamjolt Hostelry. The owner is currently away, and the business hours will be updated later. Please leave a message if you have any inquiries.
「呐…我想您呀，您听见了吗？」
"Hey... I miss you. Can you hear me?"
「回来吧酒吧店主，我最骄傲的团长，历历在目的糖浆，汽水依旧在流淌…」
"Please come back, bar owner. You're the leader that I'm the proudest of. The syrup is still there, and the soda is still flowing..."
「汪！我知道店主在哪，我们去把她找回来！！」
"Woof! I know where the bar owner is. Let's bring her back!"
欢迎光临「惊梦酒吧」。店主近期不在，营业时间另行通知。有事请留言。
Welcome to Dreamjolt Hostelry. The owner is currently away, and the business hours will be updated later. Please leave a message if you have any inquiries.
「呜呜…还没营业吗，还没营业吗。再不开门的话，我就要哭出来了哦？」
"Boohoo... Is the bar still closed? I'm going to cry if it doesn't resume operation."
「下次别再让我跑空了，你这不务正业的调饮师！」
"Don't make me come here for nothing again, you lazy bartender!"
「闭店期间，我会照顾怪物们。别担心。」
"I'll look after the monsters while the bar is closed. Don't worry."
本区域暂未开放，请不要擅自翻越护栏。
This area is closed. Please refrain from climbing over the velvet rope barrier.
禁止在酒吧内嬉戏打闹。违反者将被罚去清洁地板。
No fooling around within the bar premises. Violators will be punished to mop the floor.
空荡荡的舞台，倒也符合酒吧现在冷清的气氛。
An empty stage. It suits the currently desolate ambiance in the bar.
看起来昂贵的木制柜台，不知为何留下了许多齿痕与爪印。
An expensive-looking wooden counter. For some reason, it is covered with bite marks and claw prints.
你不禁开始想象，若是灯光骤然亮起，酒客推门而至，会是怎样热闹而欢快的场面。
You can't help picturing how lively and joyful the bar would be if the lights came on and the patrons walked in through the door now.
酒柜的装饰极为考究，每个细节都经过精心设计。那位店主的品味想必很不错。
The decoration on the liquor cabinet is exquisitely made. Every detail of the cabinet is well-designed. The bar owner must have great taste.
猎犬无神的眼底散发着品红色的光芒。这视线令你毛骨悚然。
The Hound's glassy eyes emit a sanguine glow, creating an eerie sensation.
正当你想要伸出手触碰这尊雕像时，它竟在转瞬之间消失得无影无踪。
As you attempt to touch the statue, it suddenly vanishes into thin air.
在这座充满欢闹与喧嚣的美梦中，这只猎犬选择保持沉默，冷眼旁观。
This Hound chooses to remain in silence amidst the lively and joyful dream.
发觉你已经注意到它，猎犬的身影一眨眼便消失了。
Upon realizing that you have noticed it, the Hound swiftly vanishes.
猎犬默默地注视着你和正在不断增殖的新生梦境——尽管它只是一尊雕像，并非真正的生命。
The Hound silently observes you and the burgeoning dreams — even though it is merely a statue.
当你和它四目相对时，这只猎犬飞也似地遁入忆域深处。
As your eyes meet, the Hound swiftly departs into the depths of the Memory Zone.
你从猎犬的视线中捕捉到一丝不易察觉的思绪——宽慰、孤独、哀伤…但为什么？
Faint traces of unspoken thoughts are discernible in the Hound's gaze — relief, loneliness, and sorrow... but why?
你陷入思索，不曾察觉这只苍老的猎犬已经杳无踪迹。
Lost in thought, you fail to notice the absence of the grizzled Hound.
你注意到一道冷冷的视线——原来是一尊猎犬雕像。
A cold presence catches your attention. It is a statue of a Hound.
有那么一瞬间，你惊觉这只猎犬的存在似乎变得不大*可靠*。下一秒，它便被扭曲的忆域吞没了。
For a moment, you are startled as the Hound's presence appears less *stable*. The next moment, it gets consumed by the distorted Memory Zone.
偌大的宅邸中，一条籍籍无名的猎犬正四处观瞧。
A noteless Hound roams the grand mansion, scanning its surroundings.
很快它便发现了你，随后变戏法般地消失在了屋檐下的阴影中。
Soon, it realizes your presence before vanishing into the shadows under the eaves.
猎犬的姿态与卡通动画中的哈努兄弟有几分神似——沉默寡言，恪尽职守——每一匹狼在被驯化之后都能获得如此可贵的品格。
The Hound's posture resembles that of Hanu from a cartoon — silent and loyal, a characteristic belonging to domesticated wolves.
机警的猎犬注意到了你，留下一道极具威胁意味的视线后便消失了。
The vigilant Hound notices your presence, casting a threatening glare before disappearing.
猎犬立于老人身侧，高昂着头颅，仿若昨日重现。
The Hound stands by the side of the old man, its head held high as if basked in its former glory.
它注视着你，笔直的身躯渐渐溶解于粘稠的忆质，眼底依旧迸射着怀疑一切的光。
It gazes at you, its solid form dissolving into the sticky memoria, and its eyes retaining a skeptical glow.
这尊雕像被放置在这里。或许它曾见证过盛会海选最欢乐的模样。
Perhaps, this statue has witnessed the most joyful moments in the auditions.
仿佛是察觉到自己的姿态与周遭格格不入，它很快消失在了你的眼前。
Sensing its incongruity with its surroundings, it quickly vanishes from your sight.
即便猎犬失去了踪影，那道令人不安的品红色视线依旧缠绕着你。
Even after the Hound has disappeared, the lingering sanguine sight continues to unsettle you.
看来它一定还在匹诺康尼的某处潜伏着——如果你想摆脱它的视线，恐怕得先找到它才行。
The Hound must still be lurking somewhere in Penacony. If you want to get rid of its gaze, you may need to locate its whereabouts first.
那道冰冷的视线彻底消失，你如释重负——这座金色的美梦里再也不会有猎犬踽踽独行的身影了。
The disturbing sight finally fades away, bringing you a sense of relief: The Hound no longer wanders around in this sweet dream.

Got to hand it to engines made with old-gen tech. Feels very different when it's running, very smooth. Like a starwhale's song. A bit louder than usual, but that's not important — as long as the model's compatible, heat dispersion and noise reduction aren't problems.

That's the earliest prototype. Barely usable, it can't be found on the market now. It's touted to be able to crack some special encrypted data. Some man in a flowy white robe wanted to buy it off me, but I refused.

This... I remember removing this off some early-era game cartridge. Virtual tech didn't exist at that time, so if you wanted to play a game, you'd have to stick this in a game console. There was some physical sword that rolled out and promoted at the same time — a pity no one knows its whereabouts.

Even if cup noodles are mass-produced with stringent factory standards, they'd still differ depending on their geography and times. Penacony's tastes are mildly sweet, while Belobog's are slightly spicier. That's why even everyday goods such as these have collector's value.
